SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.fielddevelopment.integrated

Class WellDeliverabilityCurve

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.fielddevelopment.integrated.WellDeliverabilityCurve
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class WellDeliverabilityCurve extends Object implements Serializable
Well deliverability curve - a fast, monotone surrogate of a well's combined IPR + VLP behaviour.

Commercial integrated-production tools (Petex GAP, Schlumberger Pipesim) decouple the expensive inflow/lift physics from the network pressure-flow solve by precomputing a deliverability curve (a VFP table reduced to wellhead pressure vs. surface rate). This class plays the same role inside NetworkNewtonSolver: it stores rate as a strictly decreasing function of wellhead (or sandface) back-pressure so the network solver can evaluate flows and derivatives cheaply and robustly, without re-running a full WellSystem flash inside every Jacobian perturbation.

Conventions

  • Pressures are in bara (ascending order).
  • Rates are surface volumetric rate in Sm3/day (non-increasing as pressure rises).
  • At or above the shut-in (no-flow) pressure the rate is zero.
  • Below the lowest tabulated pressure the curve is extrapolated towards the absolute open-flow potential (AOFP).

Construction

  • fromArrays(double[], double[]) - from sampled or measured (pressure, rate) pairs.
  • fromVogel(double, double) - from an absolute open-flow potential and shut-in pressure using a Vogel-like quadratic shape.
  • fromWellSystem(neqsim.process.equipment.reservoir.WellSystem, double, double, int) - by sampling a fully configured integrated well model.
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Constructor Details

    • WellDeliverabilityCurve

      public WellDeliverabilityCurve(double[] pressureBara, double[] rateSm3PerDay)
      Creates a deliverability curve from tabulated (pressure, rate) points.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - wellhead/back pressures in bara, ascending; length ≥ 2
      rateSm3PerDay - surface rate in Sm3/day for each pressure; non-increasing

  • Method Details

    • fromArrays

      public static WellDeliverabilityCurve fromArrays(double[] pressureBara, double[] rateSm3PerDay)
      Builds a curve from tabulated points.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - wellhead/back pressures in bara, ascending
      rateSm3PerDay - surface rate in Sm3/day, non-increasing
      Returns:
      a new deliverability curve

    • fromVogel

      public static WellDeliverabilityCurve fromVogel(double aofpSm3PerDay, double shutInPressureBara)
      Builds a Vogel-like deliverability curve from an open-flow potential and a shut-in pressure.

      Uses the Vogel inflow shape q/qmax = 1 - 0.2 (p/ps) - 0.8 (p/ps)^2 mapped to wellhead back-pressure, producing a smooth strictly decreasing curve that is convenient for screening studies and tests.

      Parameters:
      aofpSm3PerDay - absolute open-flow potential (rate at zero back-pressure) in Sm3/day
      shutInPressureBara - shut-in (no-flow) wellhead pressure in bara
      Returns:
      a new deliverability curve with 11 sample points

    • fromWellSystem

      public static WellDeliverabilityCurve fromWellSystem(WellSystem well, double minWellheadPressureBara, double maxWellheadPressureBara, int nPoints)
      Samples a configured WellSystem to build a deliverability curve.

      The well is run at a range of wellhead pressures and the resulting operating rate recorded. The full IPR + VLP physics is therefore captured once, then reused cheaply by the network solver. Non-converged or non-physical samples are clamped to zero.

      Parameters:
      well - a fully configured integrated well model
      minWellheadPressureBara - lowest wellhead pressure to sample in bara
      maxWellheadPressureBara - highest wellhead pressure to sample in bara
      nPoints - number of sample points (≥ 2)
      Returns:
      a deliverability curve representing the sampled well

    • rateAt

      public double rateAt(double pressureBara)
      Returns the surface rate delivered at a given wellhead/back pressure.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - back pressure in bara
      Returns:
      surface rate in Sm3/day (zero at or above shut-in pressure, never negative)

    • slopeAt

      public double slopeAt(double pressureBara)
      Returns the derivative of rate with respect to back pressure (Sm3/day per bar, negative).
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - back pressure in bara
      Returns:
      d(rate)/d(pressure) in Sm3/day/bar

    • getShutInPressure

      public double getShutInPressure()
      Returns the shut-in (no-flow) pressure.
      Returns:
      shut-in pressure in bara

    • getAbsoluteOpenFlowPotential

      public double getAbsoluteOpenFlowPotential()
      Returns the absolute open-flow potential.
      Returns:
      AOFP in Sm3/day
